title: C++: Beginner to Intermediate
summary: A modern C++ course centered on values, RAII, standard-library containers and algorithms, classes, generic programming, errors, builds and tests.

assumed_knowledge: Programming Foundations or equivalent diagnostic mastery
audience.target_capability: Independently design, build, test and debug a multi-file modern C++ application using explicit interfaces, value semantics, RAII and appropriate standard-library facilities.

includes: C++20 portable baseline with labeled C++23 additions
RAII, smart pointers and exception safety
STL containers, iterators, algorithms and lambdas
Templates and constrained generic design basics
Build, test, debug and sanitizer workflows
non_goals: Modules and coroutines as required skills

authoritative_sources.title: Current ISO C++ Status
authoritative_sources.version_or_date: C++23 published; C++26 in progress, accessed 2026-07

runtime.standard: C++20 baseline with C++23 additions labeled
runtime.execution_environment: Isolated Linux container with warnings enabled and deterministic resource limits
notes: The course teaches modern C++ directly rather than presenting C-style ownership first.
Compiler-specific extensions are excluded unless explicitly marked.

modules.title: Programs, translation and namespaces
modules.description: Build and diagnose a minimal C++ program and understand its translation units.

skills.title: Program structure and main
skills.description: Identify declarations, definitions, statements, headers and program entry.
outcomes: Write and explain a minimal standard C++ program using standard I/O.

skills.title: Compile and link model
skills.description: Relate source files, headers, object files, libraries and the One Definition Rule at an introductory level.
outcomes: Classify and repair preprocessing, compilation and linking failures.

skills.title: Namespaces and qualified names
skills.description: Use namespaces to organize APIs and avoid broad using-directives in headers.
outcomes: Resolve and correct ambiguous or hidden names using explicit qualification.

skills.title: Warnings and diagnostics
skills.description: Read template-aware diagnostics from the earliest actionable cause and keep warnings enabled.
outcomes: Repair a set of syntax, type and link errors using compiler evidence.

modules.title: Values, types and control flow
modules.description: Represent values safely and compose expressions and control structures.

skills.title: Fundamental types and initialization
skills.description: Choose types and initialize objects using syntax that exposes narrowing.
outcomes: Declare initialized Boolean, character, integer and floating objects without unjustified size assumptions.

skills.title: Expressions, operators and conversions
skills.description: Evaluate precedence, conversions, signedness and numeric boundaries deliberately.
outcomes: Trace and repair mixed-type expressions including narrowing and overflow risks.

skills.title: if and switch
skills.description: Implement complete decisions with scoped variables and clear conditions.
outcomes: Write and test boundary-complete selection logic.

skills.title: Loops and range iteration
skills.description: Choose counted, condition-controlled and range-based iteration while preserving termination and bounds.
outcomes: Implement and debug loops over numeric ranges and standard-library sequences.

modules.title: Functions, references and modular interfaces
modules.description: Design type-safe functions and organize declarations across files.

skills.title: Function contracts and return values
skills.description: Specify preconditions, outputs and effects and prefer return values for results.
outcomes: Design and implement cohesive functions with testable contracts.

skills.title: Value, reference and const-reference parameters
skills.description: Select parameter passing from ownership, optionality, mutation and cost requirements.
outcomes: Choose and justify parameter forms for scalar and class-type values.

skills.title: Overloading and default arguments
skills.description: Create coherent overload sets without ambiguous or surprising conversions.
outcomes: Resolve overload calls and repair ambiguity in a small API.

skills.title: Headers and source separation
skills.description: Separate interface from implementation using self-contained guarded headers.
outcomes: Create a multi-file function library with correct declarations and definitions.

modules.title: Classes and value semantics
modules.description: Model valid domain values with encapsulated invariants and predictable copying.

skills.title: Class interface and invariants
skills.description: Hide representation and expose operations that preserve valid state.
outcomes: Design a class whose constructor and methods maintain stated invariants.

skills.title: Constructors and initialization
skills.description: Initialize members in declaration order and distinguish default, converting and explicit construction.
outcomes: Implement constructors without uninitialized members or unintended conversions.

skills.title: Copy and assignment semantics
skills.description: Reason about independent values, memberwise copying and when compiler-generated operations suffice.
outcomes: Predict and test copying and assignment for a value-like class.

skills.title: Operator overloading
skills.description: Define only conventional operators that preserve value meaning and symmetry.
outcomes: Implement and test comparison or arithmetic operators with unsurprising semantics.

modules.title: RAII, ownership and exceptions
modules.description: Bind resources to object lifetimes and maintain safety through failures.

skills.title: RAII and deterministic cleanup
skills.description: Acquire resources in constructors and release them in destructors or standard wrappers.
outcomes: Refactor manual cleanup into an exception-safe RAII design.

skills.title: unique_ptr, shared_ptr and non-owning access
skills.description: Prefer unique ownership and use shared ownership only when the lifetime model requires it.
outcomes: Choose and justify an ownership representation without unnecessary shared ownership.

skills.title: Exceptions and guarantees
skills.description: Throw meaningful failures, catch at responsible boundaries and preserve invariants.
outcomes: Implement an operation with a stated basic or strong exception-safety guarantee.

skills.title: Move semantics basics
skills.description: Recognize moved-from valid states and allow efficient resource transfer without premature optimization.
outcomes: Trace copy versus move operations and use standard moves only when ownership transfer is intended.

modules.title: Composition and polymorphism
modules.description: Use composition by default and runtime polymorphism when substitutable interfaces are required.

skills.title: Composition and delegation
skills.description: Build behavior from owned member objects with explicit responsibilities.
outcomes: Refactor an inheritance-shaped design into composition when substitution is not needed.

skills.title: Public inheritance and substitution
skills.description: Use inheritance only for a valid is-a relationship and maintain base-class contracts.
outcomes: Evaluate whether derived objects safely substitute for a base interface.

skills.title: Virtual dispatch and abstract interfaces
skills.description: Define virtual operations, abstract bases and virtual destructors for polymorphic ownership.
outcomes: Implement and trace runtime dispatch through a small abstract interface.

skills.title: Slicing and safe downcasts
skills.description: Avoid object slicing and minimize checked downcasts through better interface design.
outcomes: Diagnose slicing and replace an unsafe cast with polymorphic behavior or a checked alternative.

modules.title: Standard-library data structures
modules.description: Choose and use standard containers instead of manual storage management.

skills.title: string, array and vector
skills.description: Use primary sequence types with bounds, capacity and invalidation awareness.
outcomes: Select and implement with string, array or vector for a stated ownership and size requirement.

skills.title: map, unordered_map and set
skills.description: Use ordered and hashed associative containers with suitable keys and operations.
outcomes: Choose and use an associative container while explaining ordering and expected lookup cost.

skills.title: Stacks, queues and optional values
skills.description: Use container adaptors and explicit absence rather than sentinel misuse.
outcomes: Implement a small workflow with stack, queue or optional and test empty-state behavior.

skills.title: Iterators and invalidation
skills.description: Traverse iterator ranges and reason about operations that invalidate iterators and references.
outcomes: Repair an invalidated-iterator defect and document the governing container operation.

modules.title: Algorithms, lambdas and ranges
modules.description: Express data processing through library algorithms and local callable behavior.

skills.title: Standard algorithms
skills.description: Use find, count, transform, remove-style and sorting algorithms over ranges.
outcomes: Replace a hand-written traversal with a clear standard algorithm and test it.

skills.title: Lambdas and captures
skills.description: Define local callables and capture only intended state with explicit lifetime reasoning.
outcomes: Implement and trace value and reference captures in an algorithm call.

skills.title: Comparators and ordering
skills.description: Supply strict weak orderings and avoid inconsistent comparison behavior.
outcomes: Design and test a comparator for sorting and associative lookup.

skills.title: Range-based composition basics
skills.description: Read and use simple C++20 range views without hiding ownership or lifetime problems.
outcomes: Build and explain a simple filtered and transformed range pipeline.

modules.title: Templates and generic code
modules.description: Write modest generic functions and types with explicit requirements.

skills.title: Function templates
skills.description: Generalize an operation over types while relying only on documented requirements.
outcomes: Implement and instantiate a type-safe generic function for multiple value types.

skills.title: Class templates
skills.description: Parameterize a small value or container-like type without duplicating implementation.
outcomes: Implement and test a small class template with clear value semantics.

skills.title: Conceptual constraints and diagnostics
skills.description: State template requirements and read substitution diagnostics from the first failed requirement.
outcomes: Explain why an instantiation fails and improve its requirement or call site.

skills.title: variant and type-safe alternatives
skills.description: Represent a closed union of alternatives and handle every active case.
outcomes: Model and visit a small variant without unsafe type punning.

modules.title: Build, test and debug modern C++
modules.description: Ship a maintainable command-line project with reproducible builds and tool evidence.

skills.title: Streams, files and parsing
skills.description: Use stream state and structured parsing without silently accepting malformed input.
outcomes: Implement file-backed input and output with explicit failure handling.

skills.title: Unit and integration tests
skills.description: Design deterministic tests for values, classes, exceptions and file boundaries.
outcomes: Create a focused automated test suite with normal, boundary and failure cases.

skills.title: CMake project structure
skills.description: Declare targets, include paths, warnings and test execution reproducibly.
outcomes: Configure and build a multi-target CMake project without global mutable build settings.

skills.title: Debugger, sanitizers and final project
skills.description: Combine tests, debugger state and sanitizer reports to repair lifetime, bounds and undefined-behavior defects.
outcomes: Deliver and explain a tested multi-file CLI application with no known sanitizer findings.

exit_outcomes: Build and diagnose a portable multi-file modern C++ application with reproducible configuration.
Model values and services using encapsulated classes, composition, RAII and explicit ownership.
Use standard containers, algorithms, lambdas and modest templates rather than manual low-level reinvention.
Demonstrate correctness and lifetime safety through tests, debugger evidence and sanitizers.
